context: Prevalence:   0.1-0.7% Cause:   hematogenous spread in sepsis (75%), penetrating trauma (15%), infarction (10%) Predisposed:   endocarditis, drug abuse, penetrating trauma, neoplasm, sickle cell disease √  irregular borders without capsule √  gas bubbles within abscess √  rim enhancement Rx:   76% success rate for percutaneous drain Organism:   fungus (especially Candida, Aspergillus, Cryptococcus) Prevalence:   26% of splenic abscesses Predisposed:   immunocompromised patient √  hepatosplenomegaly √  multiple round hypoechoic / hypoattenuating "target" lesions of 5-10 mm often associated with
